title: Reconsolidation merge strips manual source_kind protection on a default-ON path

findingDetails.evidence: executeMerge builds the merged successor with a hardcoded `source_kind: 'system'` (line 324) and calls `retirePredecessorForActiveReindex(db, existingMemory.id)` at line 332 while DISCARDING its return value. retirePredecessorForActiveReindex (lineage-state.ts:1392) computes a RetiredPredecessorCarry {importanceTier, sourceKind:'human'} for manual predecessors precisely so the successor can keep protection (asserted by source-kind-safety.vitest.ts:61-62). Reconsolidation is default ON (search-flags.ts:218-221, SPECKIT_RECONSOLIDATION default TRUE). The merge fires for the top folder-scoped similar memory at similarity>=0.88 regardless of its source_kind, so a human-authored predecessor is a reachable merge target. The merged tier IS carried (line 297) but source_kind is not.
findingDetails.why: The merged active row keeps a human-set importance_tier but is relabeled source_kind='system', so it becomes auto-promotable (auto-promotion.ts:178/354) and automated-overwriteable when the original human row was not. This silently violates the 'automated-writers-never-overwrite-manual' invariant the carry mechanism and its test were built to uphold; the protection is path-dependent (honored in memory-save.ts:2655 but not here).
findingDetails.recommendation: Capture the carry: `const carry = retirePredecessorForActiveReindex(db, existingMemory.id);` and when non-null set the merged insert's `source_kind` to `carry.sourceKind` (and tier to `carry.importanceTier`) instead of the unconditional 'system', mirroring memory-save.ts:2655-2660. Add an integration test that merges a human predecessor and asserts the successor remains source_kind='human'.

title: Append-version save/supersede paths discard the reindex-retire manual-source_kind carry

findingDetails.evidence: updateExistingMemory (pe-gating.ts:351) and createMemoryRecord (create-record.ts:327) both call retirePredecessorForActiveReindex and ignore the returned carry, then call applyWriteProvenance (pe-gating.ts:381, create-record.ts:368) which derives source_kind via deriveSourceKindFromContext (write-provenance.ts:82-106) from ONLY the incoming write's source/actor/tool/scope — it never inspects the retired predecessor. So an automated memory_save whose PE-gate decision UPDATEs/supersedes a human same-path or semantically-matched predecessor re-stamps the new active row source_kind='agent'/'system'. These append-version paths also bypass the in-place field guard buildGuardedUpdateParams (memory-crud-update.ts:100-145), and no other manual-overwrite gate exists in the save flow (grep of memory-save.ts shows the carry at line 2655 is the only source_kind protection).
findingDetails.why: A human-authored record superseded by an automated writer through these paths silently loses its source_kind='human' overwrite protection — the exact regression the carry return was added to prevent. Only the memory-save.ts inline same-path reindex consumes the carry; the PE-gate update and create-record lineage paths do not, leaving the protection inconsistent across reindex-retire callers.
findingDetails.recommendation: Thread the carry through both call sites: capture the RetiredPredecessorCarry and, when non-null, persistSourceKind(database, nextMemoryId, carry.sourceKind) (and re-apply carry.importanceTier) after applyWriteProvenance, exactly as memory-save.ts:2655-2660 does. Consider making retirePredecessorForActiveReindex's contract harder to misuse (e.g., a helper that retires-and-restamps the successor in one call).
